name: Weights & Biases
what_it_does: ML experiment tracking and model management platform that logs training metrics, visualizes model performance, manages model artifacts, and monitors LLM applications in production via a REST API and Python SDK.
use_cases: Logging training runs, hyperparameters, and metrics for ML model development
Comparing experiment results across runs to identify optimal model configurations
Managing model artifacts and versioning via the model registry
Monitoring LLM application quality and costs in production via Weave (W&B's LLM product)
Automating hyperparameter sweeps and surfacing results via API
not_for: Production infrastructure monitoring (use Datadog or Prometheus for ops metrics)
Teams not doing ML model training or LLM application development
Simple data science notebooks without experiment comparison needs
best_when: You're training ML models or building LLM applications and need experiment tracking, artifact management, and collaborative result sharing across a data science team.
avoid_when: You're building non-ML applications, or your ML workflow is simple enough that local logging and manual comparison is sufficient.

auth.notes: API keys are user-scoped and set via WANDB_API_KEY environment variable. Service accounts are available for CI/CD and automation. No fine-grained permission scoping per key.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited runs for individuals, 100GB storage
pricing.notes: Free tier is very generous for individual ML practitioners. Teams plan required for shared workspaces and advanced features.

performance.rate_limits: No prominently documented rate limits; SDK handles batching and retries automatically

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK surfaces clear error messages. GraphQL API errors require parsing the errors array. REST API errors include detail field.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Run initialization is not idempotent — calling wandb.init() with the same run ID resumes or creates. Artifact logging is idempotent with same version hash.
known_agent_gotchas: W&B uses both a REST API and a GraphQL API — most programmatic access goes through GraphQL, not REST
Run IDs are auto-generated unless specified — agents creating runs should set id explicitly for reproducibility
Artifact dependencies create a versioned DAG — deleting artifacts may break downstream lineage
Sweep agents consume compute as long as they're running — always set a termination condition
Weave (LLM monitoring) is a separate product layer — tracing requires different SDK calls than standard wandb logging
